title: Sports and Control
subtitle: The Velvet Cage; a theory of modern voluntary control systems
description: An artifact attempting to articulate a theory of modern voluntary control systems through the lens of professional sports leagues. It identifies different modes of extraction—body, soul, time, and autonomy—as metaphors for functionally totalizing structures that shape the individual while maintaining the illusion of freedom.

motifMap.meaning: Systems that are technically voluntary but functionally totalizing.
The differing modes of extracting body, soul, time, and autonomy from the individual.

excerpt: They don’t take your freedom—they make you spend it all in one place.

integrity.notes: Generated based on Phase Analysis 3.0. Artifact identifies a shift from internal struggle to systemic external mapping. Filename: sports-and-control.phase-the-child-of-the-call.metadata.v2.6.1.json
